# BINGO Telescope Project

BINGO (Baryon Acoustic Oscillations from Integrated Neutral Gas Observations) is a unique radio telescope designed to map the distribution of neutral hydrogen in the universe. It aims to explore baryon acoustic oscillations (BAO) to better understand dark energy and the history of our universe. 

Located in Paraíba, Brazil, the telescope observes the sky between 980 and 1260 MHz, corresponding to a redshift range of 0.13 to 0.45.
